source.title: Prototype de-identified chest pain case
source.citation: Local prototype case file for Diagnostician development.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled for prototype validation.

case.title: Acute pleuritic chest pain after travel
case.chief_complaint: Sudden pleuritic chest pain and shortness of breath.
teaching_points: Pleuritic chest pain with dyspnea and tachycardia should prompt structured risk assessment for venous thromboembolic disease.
A normal chest radiograph does not exclude a vascular cause of acute dyspnea.
Definitive review should connect the final diagnosis to the source-backed CTA and risk-factor findings.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports sudden sharp chest pain that worsens with deep inspiration, along with shortness of breath.

provenance.quote: Sudden pleuritic chest pain and shortness of breath.

facts.label: Symptom onset
facts.value: Symptoms began abruptly about two hours before arrival.

provenance.quote: Abrupt onset two hours before evaluation.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: She denies fever, productive cough, and hemoptysis.

provenance.quote: No fever, no productive cough, no hemoptysis.

facts.label: Recent travel
facts.value: She returned yesterday from a long-haul flight and reports sitting for most of the trip.

provenance.quote: Returned yesterday from a long-haul flight.

facts.label: Medication history
facts.value: She takes a combined estrogen-containing oral contraceptive.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Heart rate is 118/min, blood pressure 126/78 mmHg, respiratory rate 24/min, oxygen saturation 93% on room air, temperature 37.1 C.

facts.label: Cardiopulmonary exam
facts.value: Lungs are clear to auscultation. Heart rhythm is regular but tachycardic. No unilateral leg swelling is documented.

provenance.quote: Clear lungs, regular tachycardia, no unilateral leg swelling.

facts.label: CBC and chemistry
facts.value: CBC and basic chemistry are within reference limits.

provenance.quote: CBC and chemistry within reference limits.

facts.label: D-dimer
facts.value: D-dimer is elevated at 2.8 mg/L FEU.

facts.label: Troponin
facts.value: High-sensitivity troponin is not elevated on the initial sample.

facts.label: Chest radiograph
facts.value: Chest radiograph shows no focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax.

provenance.quote: No focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax.

facts.label: CT pulmonary angiography
facts.value: CT angiography shows segmental intraluminal filling defects in the right lower-lobe pulmonary arterial branches.

provenance.quote: Segmental intraluminal filling defects in right lower-lobe pulmonary arterial branches.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A vascular cause of pleuritic chest pain should remain on the differential with this risk-factor pattern.

provenance.quote: Risk-factor pattern supports vascular differential consideration.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Focus on causes of pleuritic chest pain with tachycardia, mild hypoxemia, and recent immobility.

provenance.quote: Pleuritic pain, tachycardia, hypoxemia, recent immobility.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Pulmonary embolism supported by compatible symptoms, risk factors, and CT angiography findings.
